titleStmt.title: Letter to the Chief Secretary's Office from Richard Cronin, 22 June 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Richard Cronin

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter to the Chief Secretary's office from Richard Cronin. In this letter Cronin is requesting financial compensation for expenses incurred while acting as a crown witness.

correspAction.placeName: Aldergrove, College Road, Co. Cork, Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Marginal note written sideways on left hand side I Ack.
II Mr Gleeson
Crown Solicitor
to report E oF 24â6â16 Aldergrove, College Road, Co. Cork, June 22 & 16. Dear Sir, I would humbly
bring under your notice the
following case: I was summoned
some months ago, as crown witness
to Clonmel. I sent Mr. Gleeson,
Crown Solicitor for that Rising my
list of expenses. He forwarded ten
shillings for hotel expenses, the police
gave railway expenses â about Â£1;
there remains beside Â£1 given to
clergyman to supply the morning
service only, also Â£2 for absence
through the whole day from
professional duties. This Â£3 has
not as yet been forwarded.
I would be grateful if you would
give the matter your kind
attention. Apologizing for
causing you trouble in the matter. I remain,
Yours very Sincerely, Richard Cronin
